member.name: Joe Robertson
member.role: MP for Isle of Wight East
member.synopsis: Joe Robertson is the Conservative MP for Isle of Wight East , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Driving Licences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 2 July 2026 to Question 13942, what estimate she has made of the average cost to an individual of maintaining licences and certifications required to drive a (i) bus and (ii) coach com
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further to costs set out in PQ answer 13942, the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency does not charge a fee for the renewal of licences to drive buses and coaches. The cost of the medical report which must be submitted with the licence application is a mat...

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 12027 on HM Coastguard, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of amending legislation to facilitate continued allowances for the Coastguard Rescu
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.heading: Car Sharing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of (a) regulation and (b) insurance on the development of the peer-to-peer car sharing sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Better Connected: A Strategy for Integrated Transport sets out this Government’s ambition to scale up shared mobility. We have committed to publishing new guidance later this year for Local Authorities on actions they can take to support car sharing, whic...

writtenQuestions.heading: South West Main Line
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many scheduled passenger train services South Western Railway operated in the 12 months ending (a) 25 May 2025 and (b) the latest 12-month period for which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: According to industry performance data, South Western Railway operated approximately 529,914 scheduled passenger train services in the 12 months ending 25 May 2025. For the latest available 12‑month period, South Western Railway operated approximately 528...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Department for Transport guidance asks Local Transport Authorities to consider car sharing when they produce their Local Transport Plans. Better Connected: A Strategy for Integrated Transport sets out our ambition to scale up shared mobility and we will p...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Taxation

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting motorists to transition to electric vehicles. This is why at Autumn Budget 2024, the Government announced new Company Car Tax rates for the years 2028-29 and 2029-30, which maintain generous incentives to support ...

writtenQuestions.heading: A46: Newark
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the total expenditure by (a) her Department and (b) National Highways was on the A46 Newark Bypass scheme prior to the cancellation of the Skanska contract.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.heading: Structures Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many bridge, tunnel and other transport structure repair projects have been assessed as ineligible for funding from the Structures Fund because they could not be completed by 31 March 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.answerText: Specialist resource, such as airspace designers, for the delivery of Airspace Modernisation are provided by private companies such as NATS. NATS is a private company, and it is for its management to decide how to resource its operation and provide its ser...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hammersmith Bridge: Closures

writtenQuestions.answerText: As the asset owner and project lead, it would be for the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham to carry out any impact assessments related to the Hammersmith Bridge restoration project. It is for Transport for London, as London's Strategic Transport Au...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Cycleways

writtenQuestions.answerText: A list of the organisations consulted during the preparation of the statutory guidance is available at: www.gov.uk/government/publications/floating-bus-stops-provision-and-design/floating-bus-stops-provision-and-design#consultation-requirements. Individua...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the annual cost to the Exchequer of applying a zero rate of VAT to commercial pilot training undertaken in the UK by (a) British nationals, (b) overseas nationals and (c) all trainee co
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has not made an estimate. I refer the Honourable Member to my answer of 23 April to the Honourable Member for Basildon and Billericay (UIN 128427), in which I stated that HMRC does not hold information on VAT revenue from pilot training, an...

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Traffic Control: Vocational Guidance

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport is committed to encouraging, inspiring, and creating opportunities for young people to pursue careers in the aviation sector, including as air traffic controllers, through its Generation Aviation Programme. The Reach for the S...

writtenQuestions.answerText: New rules of origin requirements for electric vehicles under the TCA are scheduled to come into force on 1st January 2027. We are aware of the understandable and growing concern from industry about the potential impact of the upcoming rule changes on both...

writtenQuestions.answerText: New rules of origin requirements for electric vehicles under the TCA are scheduled to come into force on 1st January 2027. We are aware of the understandable and growing concern from industry about the potential impact of the upcoming rule changes on both...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has made an estimate of the annual cost to the transport sector of compliance with Driver Certificate of Professional Competence requirements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department’s most recent published estimate of the annual cost of Driver Certificate of Professional Competence (CPC) requirements was made in the 2016 Post Implementation Review, which estimated the average annual cost of the regulations at £149 mill...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Highways: Public Relations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether any part of National Highways' external affairs function has as one of its objectives engagement with Ministers, special advisers or officials within her Department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways engages regularly with the Department’s Ministers, special advisers and officials on a wide range of matters relating to its responsibility for the strategic road network. The National Highways external affairs function does not have a s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arms Length Bodies: Lobbying
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether his Department monitors compliance by arm's length bodies with Cabinet Office guidance on the use of public funds for lobbying.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office sets standards for public bodies, including the Code of Conduct for Board Members of Public Bodies, which states that it is an improper use of public funds for public bodies to employ consultants or other companies to lobby Parliament, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cycling: Pedestrian Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many fixed penalty notices were issued for cycling on a footway in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Fixed Penalty Notices (FPNs) are matters for the Home Office. They do not however hold information on the number of FPNs issued for cycling on the footway.

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many Coastguard Rescue Officers there were in each month since January 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For each month since January 2024, there has been around 3,000 Coastguard Rescue Officers. There has been around 6% to 13% variance over this time period.
